validation_model.goal: feature and workflow claims should be backed by executable acceptance-first truth, with static guards retained only where they prevent overclaiming or protect repo/product contract surfaces

truth_definitions.shared_acceptance_harness: Reusable subsystem acceptance suites and common compile/runtime drill entrypoints such as scripts/shared_compiler_runtime_acceptance_harness.py.
truth_definitions.public_workflow_validate_actions: Named validate-* and test-* actions in scripts/objc3c_public_workflow_runner.py and package.json that compose live steps into operator-facing workflows.
truth_definitions.integration_validators: Executable integration scripts, usually check_objc3c_*_integration.py, that prove the live in-repo path rather than static structure.
truth_definitions.runnable_end_to_end_validators: Executable staged or packaged end-to-end scripts, usually check_objc3c_runnable_*_end_to_end.py or equivalent e2e flows, that prove runnable distribution behavior.
truth_definitions.retained_static_policy_guards: Static checks allowed only when they guard repo shape, docs, product layout, source-of-truth contract roots, schema validity, or task-hygiene budgets that executable flows do not replace cleanly.

requirements: New validation entrypoints must attach to a shared harness or a public workflow action before they are treated as canonical truth.
Executable validation evidence outranks static checks when proving feature behavior or integration success.
Retained static guards must map to one approved retention class and name the irreplaceable contract they protect.
Human summaries may quote validation counts only from replayable snapshots generated in the same change set.

state_rules.active: The surface is still part of the canonical validation path and remains named by a shared harness or public workflow action.
state_rules.migration-only: The surface still runs, but only as a bridge to a shared acceptance-first entrypoint and must name that successor.
state_rules.archival: The surface is retained only for historical replay or reference and must not be invoked by default CI or public docs.
state_rules.prohibited: The surface must not be reintroduced or referenced as current truth; task-hygiene or closeout gates should fail if it returns.

requirements: Every legacy check_* surface outside the retained static guard classes must be classified into one lifecycle state.
Migration-only surfaces must name a shared-harness or public-workflow successor.
Archival and prohibited surfaces must not remain in default package scripts, default CI schedules, or public command docs.
Prefer extending scripts/objc3c_public_workflow_runner.py execute_registered_action() compositions over adding milestone-local wrapper commands.
Prefer extending scripts/shared_compiler_runtime_acceptance_harness.py or another shared executable harness over creating one-off validators.
Keep retained static guards narrowly scoped to contract validation; do not use them to claim runtime or integration behavior.
When an executable validator remains necessary, wire it through validate-* or test-* actions instead of leaving it orphaned.
CI schedules must express acceptance, integration, runnable, and retained-static layers without creating a second taxonomy.
non_goals: This policy does not delete executable validators by itself.
This policy does not rename package scripts or CI jobs yet; follow-on M313 issues do that work.
This policy does not lower the public claim surface below the evidence; unresolved gaps remain fail-closed until executable proof exists.

direct_follow_on_work.M313-B002: Consolidate subsystem execution into shared harnesses and public workflow composition.
direct_follow_on_work.M313-B003: Classify legacy check_* surfaces into active, migration-only, archival, or prohibited namespaces.
direct_follow_on_work.M313-C001: Define one replayable artifact schema for acceptance evidence, migration state, and retained-guard reporting.
